title: Implement StickySpaces MVP per tech spec
description: Track implementation of docs/stickyspaces-tech-spec.md delivery plan with dependencies and acceptance gates.
acceptance_criteria: Delivery tasks Phase 0A/0B and Tasks 1-7 are completed with all documented acceptance criteria satisfied.

title: Phase 0A: Risk spike and contract gates
description: Execute pre-build experiments for workspace routing, keystroke routing, latency, launch identity, canvas overlay behavior, and mode decision freeze.
acceptance_criteria: All Phase 0A gates pass 10/10 each (50/50 total) and ADR records selected transition mode plus MVP alignment decisions.

title: Task 7: Hardening and continuous regression gates
description: Add prerequisite diagnostics, stress and chaos suites, protocol/lock behavior checks, lifecycle and local-only guardrails, readability gate, and nightly performance gates.
acceptance_criteria: Hardening suite passes three consecutive runs without flakes and nightly regressions block release candidates.

title: Post-MVP: Resolve multi-display canvas grouping strategy
description: Decide whether post-MVP multi-display uses per-display clusters or a global canvas with display badges; record decision before multi-display implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Decision documented and linked to follow-on implementation issues.

title: Phase 0B: Foundation vertical slice and reliability baseline
description: Build SPM targets, core models/store, IPC protocol/server, yabai query path, baseline CLI commands, capability probe/degraded mode, and initial unit+E2E smoke tests.
acceptance_criteria: stickyspaces new/list/status/verify-sync work on active Space; yabai failure is actionable and non-crashing.

title: Task 1: Editable text and immediate focus
description: Implement editable StickyTextView, guaranteed focus-on-create behavior, and stickyspaces edit/client.edit path with unit and E2E coverage.
acceptance_criteria: New sticky is immediately editable without app activation and edit command updates text deterministically.

title: Task 2: Workspace binding and lifecycle
description: Implement workspace monitor/reconciler, two-phase deletion, atomic create binding guard, visibility fallback strategy, and explicit single-display mode handling with tests.
acceptance_criteria: Rapid switching converges correctly, destroyed-space deletion is confirmed and exactly-once, unsupported mode is structured, and startup warnings are explicit.

title: Task 3: Position and resize
description: Add drag-to-reposition/resize with StickyStore persistence and CLI move/resize/get operations with test coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Position and size round-trip via UI and CLI with under 1pt drift.

title: Task 4: Multiple stickies and dismiss
description: Support multiple stickies per Space, hover dismiss affordance, and dismiss/dismiss-all commands with store/query verification tests.
acceptance_criteria: Create three stickies, dismiss one, and confirm exactly two remain in store plus yabai query.

title: Task 5a: Canvas layout and static rendering
description: Implement canvas layout persistence/engine, static canvas rendering with zoom-pan, active workspace highlighting, and zoom-out/canvas-layout commands.
acceptance_criteria: zoom-out shows all supported Spaces, active highlight is correct, and layout remains deterministic over repeated runs.

title: Task 5b: Canvas interaction
description: Implement sticky-click navigation, region rearrangement persistence, zoom-in path, move-region command, and topology-snapshot-driven rendering with tests.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking sticky reliably navigates to target Space and region arrangement persists across repeated runs.

title: Task 6: Zoom transitions and mode fallback
description: Finalize transition orchestration for selected mode, add forced-mode parity checks, validate panel-canvas alignment, and implement bounded liveness fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Continuity gates pass, transition duration is 300-500ms p95, and parity passes for both modes when dual-mode is enabled.

title: Story 3: Deliver Map Overview zoom-out
description: Implement Story 3 from docs/map-overview-zoom-out-tech-spec.md across snapshot correctness, transition continuity, region rearrangement, and hardening.
acceptance_criteria: All Story 3 requirement IDs in the spec are covered by passing tests and deterministic automation evidence.

title: Phase 0: Risk probe transition continuity
description: Wire prepare+animate overview path end-to-end and validate first-frame continuity plus duration envelope.
acceptance_criteria: Journey test passes with artifacts; no blank/stuck overlay; Frame A/B identity gate passes; initial duration samples are within target envelope.

title: Phase 1: Snapshot correctness for all workspaces
description: Ensure snapshot includes all supported workspaces (including empty), active highlight correctness, screenshot-thumbnail fidelity, and deterministic outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Region count matches workspace count, active highlight is unique/correct, and repeated unchanged snapshots are deterministic.

title: Phase 2: Region rearrangement session persistence
description: Support move-region path and session-scoped persistence via canvas layout with deterministic post-move behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Moved coordinates are reflected in canvas-layout reads and persist across repeated overview invocations in-session.

title: Phase 3: Story 3 hardening and quality gates
description: Add missing Story 3 tests and degraded-mode behavior checks; enforce read-only state guarantees for overview entry/render.
acceptance_criteria: Coverage matrix has no uncovered Story 3 requirements; added tests pass 3 consecutive runs without flakes; no persistent user-visible state mutation.

title: Map Overview workspace cards render abstract placeholders instead of scaled desktop screenshots
description:
## Summary

The Map Overview Frame C visual contract (tech spec line 70) states:

> "each workspace card in Frame C should be a scaled thumbnail of the workspace's real desktop appearance at zoom time, not an abstract placeholder card"

ZO-FR-3 reinforces this: users should "see each supported workspace as a distinct bordered region rendered as a scaled screenshot thumbnail of that workspace's real desktop appearance (windows, wallpaper, and sticky placement)."

Currently, workspace cards render as flat colored rectangles with a text label and small yellow sticky-marker dots — exactly the "abstract placeholder card" the spec says they should NOT be.

## Current behavior

- `drawRegion()` in `ZoomOutOverviewPresenter.swift` fills each workspace card with a single solid color (blue for active, dark gray for inactive), draws a "Workspace N" label, and renders sticky previews as small yellow rounded rects.
- A single full-screen desktop screenshot IS captured during `prepare()` and drawn as the view background, but it is never decomposed into per-workspace thumbnails rendered inside cards.
- `CanvasThumbnailMetadata` on each region tracks metadata (source, displayID, capturedAt) but carries no actual image data.
- The default thumbnail source is `.synthetic` (confirmed by `zoomOutSnapshotUsesSyntheticThumbnailMetadataByDefault`).

## Expected behavior (acceptance criteria)

1. When a knowledge worker opens Map Overview, each workspace card shows a recognizable scaled-down version of that workspace's actual desktop content (visible windows, wallpaper, sticky placement) — not a flat colored rectangle.
2. A workspace the knowledge worker has never visited (empty workspace) still appears as a bordered region, but is visually distinct from workspaces with content.
3. The active workspace card remains identifiable at a glance (highlight/border treatment is preserved on top of the thumbnail).
4. Repeated zoom-out invocations with unchanged workspace content produce visually stable cards — thumbnails do not flicker, swap, or degrade across invocations within a session.
5. If a desktop screenshot cannot be obtained for a workspace (e.g., screen capture permission denied), the card falls back gracefully to the current synthetic rendering rather than showing a blank or broken card.

## Existing E2E test gap

`zoomOutFinalFrameShowsScaledWorkspaceScreenshots` exists but currently passes with the synthetic rendering because its assertions use visual-richness proxies (`quantizedColorCount >= 18`, `luminanceStdDev >= 6`) that the colored rectangles already satisfy. The test also checks `thumbnail.displayID != nil` which verifies metadata, not actual rendered screenshot content. This test should be strengthened to fail when cards lack real desktop screenshot content.

## Key files

- `Sources/StickySpacesApp/ZoomOutOverviewPresenter.swift` — `drawRegion()` method (line ~389) is the render path to change; `prepare()` method (line ~168) already captures a full-screen screenshot that could be a starting point.
- `Sources/StickySpacesShared/CanvasModels.swift` — `CanvasThumbnailMetadata` and `CanvasRegionSnapshot` model the per-region thumbnail contract.
- `Tests/E2E/ZoomOutCanvasOverviewJourneyTests.swift` — `zoomOutFinalFrameShowsScaledWorkspaceScreenshots` needs tightening.
- `docs/map-overview-zoom-out-tech-spec.md` — authoritative spec, see "Screen Sequence (Story 3 visual contract)" section and ZO-FR-3/D-ZO-3.
- `docs/map-overview-03-last-frame-after-zoom.svg` — Frame C mock showing the target card appearance with thumbnail backgrounds and window silhouettes.

## Design context

- Design decision D-ZO-3 ("Screenshot-thumbnail rendering contract") explicitly chose real screenshots over synthetic reconstructions to keep the overview "visually trustworthy."
- The two-step `prepare`/`animate` API (D-ZO-4) already captures a desktop screenshot at prepare time — extending this to per-workspace captures or cropping the full capture into per-workspace regions is a natural path.
- macOS workspace switching needed to capture non-active workspaces is a known constraint; cached or stale captures with structured fallback are acceptable per the thumbnail metadata design.

title: CLI client-server IPC: make stickyspaces CLI usable with lazy daemon
description:
The stickyspaces CLI currently creates a fresh in-memory DemoApp with fakes on every invocation (Sources/StickySpacesCLI/StickySpacesCLI.swift lines 13-35). No real windows appear and state is lost between commands. This epic adds a client-server IPC layer: a long-lived daemon process that owns StickyStore and creates real NSPanel windows, and a short-lived CLI client that talks to it via Unix socket. The daemon starts lazily on first use — no explicit serve command.

Tech spec: docs/cli-interface-tech-spec.md
Parent spec: docs/stickyspaces-tech-spec.md (IPC sections at lines 291-368, 444-446)
Plan: .cursor/plans/cli_client-server_ipc_75965318.plan.md

title: Phase 1: Unix socket server + client with round-trip tests
description:
Build the transport layer: UnixSocketServer (actor that accepts connections and delegates to IPCServer.handleLine) and IPCSocketClient (struct that connects, handshakes, and sends/receives IPCRequest/IPCResponse via IPCWireCodec). This is the riskiest assumption — validate that Swift concurrency can reliably drive POSIX Unix socket accept/read/write.

## Context

- Tech spec: docs/cli-interface-tech-spec.md — see 'Phase 1: Socket Server + Client' and the 'UnixSocketServer' / 'IPCSocketClient' component sections
- IPCServer already exists at Sources/StickySpacesApp/IPCServer.swift — routes IPCRequest to StickySpacesAutomationAPI. The socket server just moves bytes to/from it.
- IPCWireCodec already exists at Sources/StickySpacesShared/IPCProtocol.swift — JSON newline-delimited encode/decode for IPCRequest/IPCResponse.
- Existing tests at Tests/Integration/CLIWorkflowTests.swift (10 tests) and Tests/Integration/IPCWorkflowTests.swift (7 tests) must continue to pass.
- Follow TDD per AGENTS.md. Test guide at docs/testing.md.

## Implementation

### UnixSocketServer (new file: Sources/StickySpacesApp/UnixSocketServer.swift)

Public actor with interface:
  - init(socketPath: String, ipcServer: IPCServer)
  - func start() async throws — POSIX socket(AF_UNIX)/bind/listen/accept loop via withTaskGroup, child task per connection reads newline-delimited lines, calls ipcServer.handleLine(), writes response
  - func shutdown() async — cancel task group, close fd, unlink socket path

### IPCSocketClient (new file: Sources/StickySpacesCLI/IPCSocketClient.swift)

Public struct with interface:
  - init(socketPath: String) throws — connect to Unix socket, throw IPCSocketClientError.connectionFailed if absent
  - func send(_ request: IPCRequest) async throws -> IPCResponse — encode via IPCWireCodec, write, read response line, decode
  - First call sends .hello(protocolVersion: 1) handshake, validates server response

### TestServerEnvironment (new file: Tests/Support/ or Tests/Integration/)

Reusable helper: starts UnixSocketServer + IPCServer on a unique temp socket path (FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory + UUID), wired to in-memory StickyStore + FakeYabaiQuerying + InMemoryPanelSync.

### Tests (new file: Tests/Integration/IPCSocketRoundTripTests.swift)

Write these tests FIRST (TDD):
1. clientCreatesStickyViaSocketAndReceivesCreatedResponse — send .new(text:), assert .created with workspaceID 1
2. statePersistsAcrossSeparateClientConnections — create via one connection, list via another, see the sticky
3. serverShutdownRemovesSocketFile — after shutdown(), socket path no longer exists
4. socketRoundTripLatencyUnder200ms — 30 round-trips, p95 < 200ms
acceptance_criteria:
- clientCreatesStickyViaSocketAndReceivesCreatedResponse passes
- statePersistsAcrossSeparateClientConnections passes
- serverShutdownRemovesSocketFile passes
- All existing CLIWorkflowTests and IPCWorkflowTests still pass (swift test green)

title: Phase 2: Daemon mode with --daemon flag, instance lock, and signal cleanup
description:
Build the daemon process that hosts UnixSocketServer with real dependencies. When invoked as 'stickyspaces --daemon', the process creates the config directory, acquires an exclusive file lock, wires real AppKit panels, starts the socket server, and runs the NSApplication event loop.

## Context

- Tech spec: docs/cli-interface-tech-spec.md — see 'Phase 2: Daemon Mode + Instance Lock' and the 'Daemon Mode' component section
- Depends on Phase 1 (spacetree-t6d.1) for UnixSocketServer and IPCSocketClient
- AppKitPanelSync already exists at Sources/StickySpacesApp/AppKitPanelSync.swift — creates real NSPanel windows. It's guarded by #if canImport(AppKit).
- StickyManager, StickyStore, FakeYabaiQuerying, InMemoryPanelSync all exist in Sources/StickySpacesApp/
- The existing EntryPoint.swift (Sources/StickySpacesCLI/EntryPoint.swift) currently routes all commands to DemoApp — this task modifies it to intercept --daemon.
- Follow TDD per AGENTS.md. Test guide at docs/testing.md.

## Implementation

### Modify EntryPoint.swift

Add a check at the top of main(): if args.first == "--daemon", call startDaemon() which never returns. Leave the existing DemoApp path intact for now (Phase 3 replaces it for non-daemon commands).

### startDaemon() function (in EntryPoint.swift or new DaemonMode.swift)

1. Create ~/.config/stickyspaces/ directory (FileManager.createDirectory withIntermediateDirectories)
2. Acquire exclusive file lock on ~/.config/stickyspaces/instance.lock via POSIX open() + flock(LOCK_EX | LOCK_NB). If flock fails (EWOULDBLOCK), print 'StickySpaces daemon is already running.' to stderr and exit(1).
3. Wire real dependencies: StickyStore(), AppKitPanelSync(), StickyManager(store:yabai:panelSync:) with FakeYabaiQuerying(currentSpace: WorkspaceID(rawValue: 1)), IPCServer(manager:)
4. Create UnixSocketServer(socketPath: ~/.config/stickyspaces/sock, ipcServer:)
5. Install SIGINT/SIGTERM handlers via DispatchSource.makeSignalSource() that call unlink() on socket + lock paths, then exit(0). Note: must call signal(SIGINT, SIG_IGN) / signal(SIGTERM, SIG_IGN) before creating dispatch sources.
6. Call try await server.start() then NSApplication.shared.run()

### Tests

1. secondDaemonExitsWhenLockHeld — acquire flock on a temp lock file, then verify that a second acquisition attempt fails
2. Manual smoke test: run 'swift run stickyspaces --daemon' in a terminal, then from another terminal connect with IPCSocketClient and send a .new request. Verify response. Kill daemon with Ctrl-C, verify socket file is removed.
acceptance_criteria:
- 'stickyspaces --daemon' starts, binds socket, and responds to an IPCSocketClient connection
- Killing the daemon with SIGTERM removes the socket file and lock file
- A second 'stickyspaces --daemon' prints 'already running' and exits 1
- All existing tests still pass

title: Phase 3: DaemonLauncher, CLIClientRunner, and entry point routing
description:
Build the lazy daemon startup glue and the CLI client-side arg translation. After this task, running 'swift run stickyspaces new --text "Hello"' with no daemon running will auto-start one, create a sticky, and print the result. Subsequent commands reuse the same daemon.

## Context

- Tech spec: docs/cli-interface-tech-spec.md — see 'Phase 3: Daemon Launcher + CLI Client Runner' and the 'DaemonLauncher' / 'CLIClientRunner' / 'Entry Point Routing' component sections
- Depends on Phase 2 (spacetree-t6d.2) for the working --daemon mode
- The existing StickySpacesCLICommandRunner (Sources/StickySpacesCLI/StickySpacesCLI.swift) has arg-parsing helpers (parseOption, parseDoubleOption, parseIntOption at lines 157-176) and a command switch (lines 43-155) that translates args to automation calls. CLIClientRunner needs similar logic but translates to IPCRequest instead.
- The existing DemoApp/DemoAppFactory code in StickySpacesCLI.swift stays unchanged — it's still used by CLIWorkflowTests.
- Follow TDD per AGENTS.md. Test guide at docs/testing.md.

## Implementation

### DaemonLauncher (new file: Sources/StickySpacesCLI/DaemonLauncher.swift)

Public enum with static method:
  - ensureDaemonRunning(socketPath: String) async throws

Logic:
1. Try a probe connection (IPCSocketClient(socketPath:)). If succeeds, return (daemon running).
2. If socket file exists but connection refused, check if instance.lock is held (try flock LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB on lock file — if it succeeds, the lock was stale, unlink socket, release lock). If lock IS held, wait and retry (daemon may be starting).
3. Resolve executable path via ProcessInfo.processInfo.arguments[0] (or Bundle.main.executablePath).
4. Create Process() with arguments: [executablePath, "--daemon"]. Set standardOutput/standardError to FileHandle(forWritingAtPath: ~/.config/stickyspaces/daemon.log). Clear test env vars (STICKYSPACES_SIMULATE_YABAI_UNAVAILABLE). Call process.launch().
5. Poll socket at 50ms intervals (try await Task.sleep(for: .milliseconds(50))) for up to 3 seconds. On each tick, try IPCSocketClient probe connection.
6. If timeout, throw DaemonLaunchError.timeout with message pointing to daemon.log.

### CLIClientRunner (new file: Sources/StickySpacesCLI/CLIClientRunner.swift)

Public enum with static method:
  - run(args: [String], socketPath: String) async throws -> String

Logic:
1. Call DaemonLauncher.ensureDaemonRunning(socketPath:).
2. Create IPCSocketClient(socketPath:).
3. Translate args to IPCRequest — same switch-on-first-arg pattern as StickySpacesCLICommandRunner but returns IPCRequest instead of calling automation directly. Use same parseOption/parseDoubleOption/parseIntOption helpers (extract or duplicate — they're 20 lines total).
4. Call client.send(request).
5. Format IPCResponse as human-readable string. Match the output format of the existing StickySpacesCLICommandRunner where possible for consistency.
6. Return the string.

### Modify EntryPoint.swift

Replace the current main() body. New logic:
  - If args.first == "--daemon" → call startDaemon() (from Phase 2)
  - Otherwise → call CLIClientRunner.run(args:socketPath:defaultSocketPath()) and print result
  - defaultSocketPath() returns ~/.config/stickyspaces/sock

### Tests (new file: Tests/Integration/CLIClientModeTests.swift)

Write these tests FIRST (TDD), using TestServerEnvironment from Phase 1 (pre-started server, no DaemonLauncher involved):
1. userCreatesAndListsStickiesThroughClientRunner — CLIClientRunner.run(["new", "--text", "Sprint planning"], socketPath:) then run(["list"], socketPath:), assert list output contains 'Sprint planning'
2. daemonLauncherReusesExistingDaemon — start TestServerEnvironment, call ensureDaemonRunning, verify it returns without spawning
3. cliPrintsActionableErrorWhenLaunchFails — use a nonexistent socket path with no ability to spawn, verify error message
4. unknownCommandReturnsUsageViaSocketRoundTrip — send unknown command, verify usage output
acceptance_criteria:
- userCreatesAndListsStickiesThroughClientRunner passes (against pre-started test server)
- Running 'swift run stickyspaces new --text "Hello"' from terminal with no daemon: daemon auto-starts, sticky created, output printed
- Running 'swift run stickyspaces list' after: shows the sticky from the previous command
- All existing CLIWorkflowTests and IPCWorkflowTests still pass

title: Phase 4: Polish, hardening, and end-to-end manual validation
description:
Harden the daemon lifecycle and validate the full end-to-end experience: real NSPanel windows appearing on the macOS desktop when CLI commands are run.

## Context

- Tech spec: docs/cli-interface-tech-spec.md — see 'Phase 4: Polish + Hardening'
- Depends on Phase 3 (spacetree-t6d.3) for the complete lazy-start CLI
- AppKitPanelSync (Sources/StickySpacesApp/AppKitPanelSync.swift) creates NSPanel windows with AppKit. It calls NSApplication.shared.setActivationPolicy(.regular) and activate(ignoringOtherApps: true). This needs validation in the daemon context — a CLI binary (no .app bundle) running NSApplication.shared.run() may behave differently.
- The daemon logs to ~/.config/stickyspaces/daemon.log — if panels don't appear, this log is the first place to check.

## Tasks

1. Clear test-only environment variables (STICKYSPACES_SIMULATE_YABAI_UNAVAILABLE) when DaemonLauncher spawns the daemon process — prevent test env from leaking into production daemon.

2. Daemon log management: ensure daemon.log is created with sane permissions. Consider truncating on daemon start (or appending with timestamp header) so the log doesn't grow unbounded across restarts.

3. Optional: add a 'stickyspaces stop' command that connects to the daemon and sends a shutdown signal (or just sends SIGTERM to the daemon PID read from the lock file). Low priority — pkill stickyspaces works for MVP.

4. Manual end-to-end validation on macOS desktop:
   - Run 'swift run stickyspaces new --text "Hello world"' — verify a floating sticky note window appears
   - Run 'swift run stickyspaces new --text "Second note"' — verify a second window appears
   - Run 'swift run stickyspaces list' — verify both stickies listed
   - Run 'swift run stickyspaces dismiss-all' — verify all windows disappear
   - If windows don't appear, investigate: check daemon.log, check if setActivationPolicy(.accessory) is needed instead of .regular to avoid Dock icon, check if NSApplication.shared.run() initializes the window server connection properly from a non-bundled binary.

5. If AppKit panels don't work from a CLI binary, the mitigation is to switch to .accessory activation policy or explore wrapping the daemon in a minimal .app bundle (scripts/bundle.sh approach mentioned in the parent tech spec D-12).
acceptance_criteria:
- 'swift run stickyspaces new --text "Hello"' creates a visible floating window on the macOS desktop
- 'swift run stickyspaces dismiss-all' removes all visible windows
- Daemon log file exists at ~/.config/stickyspaces/daemon.log with readable output
- No test environment variables leak into the daemon process

title: Fix Story 3 visual regressions
description: Address regressions found in recording-enabled artifacts against docs/map-overview-zoom-out-tech-spec.md: Frame A/B identity drift and non-faithful workspace thumbnails in final overview.
acceptance_criteria: Recording-enabled Story 3 E2E tests pass and artifacts satisfy Frame A/B identity and thumbnail-fidelity contracts.

title: Bugfix: Frame A/B first-frame identity drift
description: After screenshot fidelity precondition is complete, fix prepare/first-frame behavior so Frame B is visually identical to Frame A before zoom motion starts with no visible overlay jump.
acceptance_criteria: STICKYSPACES_RUN_SCREEN_RECORDING_TESTS=1 swift test --filter zoomOutFirstFrameMatchesPreZoomFrame passes with changed-pixel ratio <= 0.1 percent and max per-channel delta <= 2.

title: Bugfix: Workspace cards must show faithful desktop thumbnails
description: After Frame A/B identity is fixed, render each workspace card from screenshot-faithful desktop thumbnails showing windows, wallpaper, and sticky placement cues.
acceptance_criteria: Recording-enabled final-frame artifact shows recognizably desktop-like workspace thumbnails and STICKYSPACES_RUN_SCREEN_RECORDING_TESTS=1 swift test --filter zoomOutFinalFrameShowsScaledWorkspaceScreenshots passes.

title: Precondition: Desktop screenshot fidelity pipeline
description: Establish a pixel-faithful screenshot capture/render path as the source of truth for overview prepare and thumbnail rendering so Story 3 visuals are based on real desktop imagery, not synthetic reconstruction.
acceptance_criteria: Prepare-time background source is faithful to desktop pixels in stable ROI; recording-enabled identity checks can rely on this source; synthetic fallback is only used when capture is unavailable.
